title: Primoris Services (PRIM) Shares Crater 50% Amid Expanded Renewables Issues – HBSS
author: Hagens Berman
description: Primoris Services Corporation (NYSE: PRIM) experienced a catastrophic 50% stock price decline on May 6, 2026, after reporting significant year-over-year and sequential revenue and gross profit declines in its Energy segment. The company revealed widespread execution issues across multiple solar projects, including project redesigns, labor problems, sequencing errors, and weather disruptions—contradicting earlier management statements that attributed margin pressures to isolated soil and rock conditions. The market reaction wiped out approximately $5.5 billion in market capitalization, prompting shareholder rights firm Hagens Berman to investigate potential disclosure inadequacies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Severe 50% stock price crash following disappointing Q1 2026 earnings, significant revenue and gross profit declines in core Energy segment, widespread project execution issues across multiple solar projects, and management credibility concerns due to contradictory statements about the scope and nature of problems.

title: BREAKING: ADMA Biologics Shares Fall Over 24%; Investors Encouraged to Contact Block & Leviton to Possibly Recover Losses
author: Block & Leviton Llp
description: ADMA Biologics shares fell over 24% after withdrawing long-term financial guidance due to market competitive dynamics and allegations of inflated growth through channel stuffing. Primoris Services stock declined over 45%, and GeneDX shares plummeted over 45% on May 5th. Block & Leviton is investigating all three companies for potential securities law violations and encouraging affected investors to seek recovery.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 24% after withdrawing financial guidance; allegations of inflated growth through channel stuffing and misleading business practices related to Asceniv product; securities investigation underway
Stock declined over 45%; subject to securities investigation by Block & Leviton for potential violations
Shares plummeted over 45% on May 5th; under investigation for potential securities law violations

title: This Infrastructure Stock Is Up 124% This Past Year, and One Fund Dumped Its $38 Million Stake Last Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Goodlander Investment Management exited its entire $37.77 million position in Primoris (PRIM) during Q4 2025, selling 275,000 shares. Despite the stock surging 124% over the past year and the company delivering solid growth with 19% revenue increase and 22% EBITDA growth, the fund's exit appears to be profit-taking after the dramatic rally rather than a rejection of the infrastructure theme, as the fund maintains other infrastructure and industrial holdings.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the company shows strong fundamentals with 19% revenue growth and 22% EBITDA growth, the stock's 124% surge has created lofty valuation expectations. The fund's exit is characterized as profit-taking rather than a fundamental concern, suggesting the company remains sound but may face near-term headwinds from stretched valuations after its dramatic rally.
Mentioned as a top holding and recommended by The Motley Fool, indicating positive regard, though the article provides no specific performance metrics or analysis.

title: Sterling (STRL) Up 47.2% YTD on Infrastructural Push: What's Next? - Zacks Investment Research
author: Zacks Investment Research
description: Sterling Infrastructure, Inc. (STRL) has seen a significant surge in its stock price, outperforming the industry and the broader market. The company's strategic focus on e-infrastructure and higher-margin projects has driven its growth, with a strong backlog and solid financial performance. However, the stock's valuation is a concern, and investors should consider the potential risks before making an investment decision.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights the company's strong performance, with its stock price surging 47.2% year-to-date and outpacing the industry and broader market. The company's strategic focus on e-infrastructure and higher-margin projects has driven its growth, with a strong backlog and solid financial performance.
The article compares Sterling Infrastructure's valuation to that of Primoris Services Corporation, noting that STRL is trading at a higher forward P/E ratio.
